Title: MuMADS1 and MaOFP1 regulate fruit quality in a tomato ovate mutant

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ovate mutant LA3543; WT Ailsa Craig LA2838A
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Starch and sucrose metabolism; cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis support via hexose phosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hexose phosphorylation linked to glycolysis and cell wall precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates glucose/hexoses to corresponding hexose phosphates for entry into central carbon metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with firmer fruit and reduced soluble carbohydrates in ovate; reduced expression in MuMADS1+MaOFP1 lines is associated with softer fruit and improved sugar accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in red mature fruit; up-regulated in WT vs ovate, implying elevated expression in ovate relative to WT
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage (red mature stage); transgene complementation in ovate background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR validation; association with fruit firmness and sugar phenotypes in transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc04g081400.2 was identified as a DEG involved in sucrose/cell wall metabolism. 2. It was up-regulated in the ovate mutant relative to WT. 3. Its expression was suppressed by co-expression of MuMADS1 and MaOFP1, consistent with decreased firmness and improved sugar status.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate sugar partitioning versus cell wall deposition for fruit texture/flavor improvement, but causal direct engineering value not fully established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a downstream target linked to firmness rather than a primary subject gene of transformation

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ovate mutant LA3543; WT Ailsa Craig LA2838A
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cell wall cellulose degradation; starch and sucrose metabolism related sugar release
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses beta-glucosidic linkages in cell wall-derived glucosides/cellulose-derived oligosaccharides to release glucose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for remodeling of cell wall and release of sugar
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glucose; fruit firmness trait via cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to flavor sweetness and softening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulation is associated with increased fruit firmness and decreased glucose in ovate; co-expression of MuMADS1 and MaOFP1 compensates this down-regulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in red mature fruit; down-regulated in ovate and restored in MuMADS1+MaOFP1 lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental stage; transgenic complementation in ovate mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR validation; metabolite/firmness association
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc07g063880.2 was down-regulated in ovate. 2. This down-regulation likely contributes to firmer fruit and lower glucose. 3. Co-expression of MuMADS1 and MaOFP1 restored its expression, supporting synergistic regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Another beta-glucosidase family member, lysosomal beta-glucosidase-like, showed a different pattern, indicating family divergence.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ovate mutant LA3543; WT Ailsa Craig LA2838A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucose to UDP-glucuronic acid with NAD+ reduction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cell wall polysaccharides; indirectly soluble sugars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation in ovate may increase cell wall polysaccharide content and fruit firmness; expression decreases in MuMADS1+MaOFP1 transformants together with reduced firmness
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in red mature fruit; elevated in ovate relative to WT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc02g067080.2 is up-regulated in ovate. 2. Its function diverts UDP-glucose toward cell wall synthesis. 3. Reduced expression in MuMADS1+MaOFP1 lines correlates with softer fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as a putative downstream target affecting firmness.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ovate mutant LA3543; WT Ailsa Craig LA2838A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucuronic acid to UDP-galacturonic acid for pectin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation in ovate may increase cell wall polysaccharides and firmness; reduced in MuMADS1+MaOFP1 transformants with softer fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in red mature fruit; elevated in ovate relative to WT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc12g010540.1 was up-regulated in ovate. 2. It likely boosts UDP-galacturonic acid supply for cell wall synthesis. 3. Its expression decreased in complemented lines, consistent with reduced firmness.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as downstream pathway gene, not a transformed focal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ovate mutant LA3543; WT Ailsa Craig LA2838A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galacturonic acid residues to synthesize homogalacturonan pectin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms structural polymer affecting target trait
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation of multiple galacturonosyltransferases is associated with increased firmness in ovate; this up-regulation is reduced by MuMADS1 and MaOFP1 transformation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in red mature fruit; higher in ovate than WT
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi-localized (inferred from discussion of pectin synthesis machinery)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR validation for Solyc06g083310.2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple galacturonosyltransferase genes were up-regulated in ovate. 2. Their expression likely contributes to increased fruit firmness via pectin synthesis. 3. MuMADS1 and MaOFP1 transformation progressively diminished this up-regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Includes both validated qRT-PCR gene Solyc06g083310.2 and additional members discussed in text.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ovate mutant LA3543; WT Ailsa Craig LA2838A
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously linked to banana fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic tomato fruit, expression increased during development and peaked at red mature stage; higher in MO cotransgenic lines than in M lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental progression (IMG, MG, BR, RM); 35S overexpression in ovate background
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms/acts in a validated protein-protein interaction with MaOFP1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation of carbohydrate and cell wall metabolism genes; synergistic coregulation with MaOFP1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sugar accumulation; starch and sucrose metabolism; carbon metabolism; cell wall metabolism; fruit softening
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: putative downstream DEGs in carbohydrate and cell wall metabolism including plastidic hexokinase (Solyc04g081400.2); putative beta-glucosidase 41 (Solyc07g063880.2); UDP-glucose 6-dehydrogenase 1 (Solyc02g067080.2); UDP-glucuronate 4-epimerase 1-like (Solyc12g010540.1); galacturonosyltransferases (Solyc06g083310.2; Solyc02g089440.2; Solyc07g055930.2); lysosomal beta-glucosidase-like (Solyc04g015560.2); pectate lyase 8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Partially restores ovate-altered expression of sugar and cell wall metabolic genes; coexpression with MaOFP1 more strongly suppresses ovate-upregulated cell-wall genes and restores downregulated sugar-remodeling genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller that partially compensates ovate defects and, together with MaOFP1, nearly restores fruit quality
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: soluble sugars (fructose, glucose, sucrose) and cell wall-related firmness trait
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: determines fruit taste, soluble solids, and texture
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in tomato ovate partially restores rounder fruit shape, lowers excessive firmness, and increases soluble solids and sugar content; co-expression with MaOFP1 gives near full recovery
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in tomato ovate mutant; qRT-PCR expression profiling; fruit quality phenotyping; RNA-seq DEG analysis; prior validated protein-protein interaction with MaOFP1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MuMADS1 overexpression in ovate partially recovered fruit shape and quality traits. 2. It reduced transcriptomic divergence from WT, especially in carbohydrate/cell-wall pathways. 3. Combined with MaOFP1, it had an additive effect and nearly fully restored sugars, soluble solids and firmness.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for engineering climacteric fruit flavor/texture, especially in combination with OFP factors; stacking with MaOFP1 appears more effective than single-gene deployment
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-gene expression gave only partial recovery; possible broad transcriptional effects due to TF activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Heterologous regulator from banana used in tomato; not homologous to tomato OVATE but shares many downstream targets with OVATE

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ovate mutant LA3543; WT Ailsa Craig LA2838A
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously implicated in banana fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic tomato fruit, expression increased during development and peaked at red mature stage; higher in MO cotransgenic lines than in O lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit developmental progression (IMG, MG, BR, RM); 35S overexpression in ovate background
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with MuMADS1 through a validated interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional/coregulatory control of carbohydrate and cell wall metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sugar accumulation; starch metabolism; starch and sucrose metabolism; carbon metabolism; cell wall metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: putative downstream DEGs in carbohydrate and cell wall metabolism including plastidic hexokinase (Solyc04g081400.2); putative beta-glucosidase 41 (Solyc07g063880.2); UDP-glucose 6-dehydrogenase 1 (Solyc02g067080.2); UDP-glucuronate 4-epimerase 1-like (Solyc12g010540.1); galacturonosyltransferases; lysosomal beta-glucosidase-like (Solyc04g015560.2)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Partially compensates ovate-driven misexpression of carbohydrate and cell wall genes; in coexpression with MuMADS1 regulates a broader DEG set than either alone
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong compensatory regulator of ovate-associated fruit quality defects; synergistic partner of MuMADS1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: soluble sugars (fructose, glucose, sucrose) and cell wall-associated firmness trait
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression partially restores fruit shape, reduces firmness, and increases soluble solids and sugars in ovate; co-expression with MuMADS1 provides stronger rescue
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in tomato ovate mutant; qRT-PCR; fruit quality phenotyping; RNA-seq DEG analysis; prior validated interaction with MuMADS1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaOFP1 partially compensated ovate defects in fruit shape and quality. 2. It altered expression of carbohydrate and cell-wall metabolism genes. 3. Co-expression with MuMADS1 generated an additive effect and more complete quality recovery.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory gene for fruit flavor/texture improvement, especially in gene-stacking strategies with MuMADS1 or related ripening regulators
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad downstream effects expected for regulatory proteins; alone gives only partial trait restoration
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: As an OFP, functionally related to OVATE family biology but introduced from banana into tomato
